paper.title: Lookahead Optimizer: k steps forward, 1 step back

claims.text: Lookahead reduces the variance of the inner optimizer's iterates and improves learning stability across hyperparameter settings
Lookahead-wrapped SGD and Adam achieve faster convergence (lower training loss at matched step budgets) than the raw inner optimizers with default hyperparameters
The computational and memory overhead of Lookahead is negligible

methods.name: Lookahead
methods.description: Maintains slow weights phi and fast weights theta. For each outer step, fast weights are updated k times using an inner optimizer A starting from current slow weights, then slow weights are interpolated toward final fast weights: phi_t = phi_{t-1} + alpha_slow * (theta_{t,k} - phi_{t-1})
methods.runnable_hint:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(0)
X = np.random.randn(100, 10)
y = np.random.randn(100, 1)
w_slow = np.zeros((10, 1))
w_fast = w_slow.copy()
k = 5
alpha_slow = 0.5
lr_inner = 0.01
for outer_step in range(20):
    w_fast = w_slow.copy()
    for i in range(k):
        pred = X @ w_fast
        loss = np.mean((pred - y)**2)
        grad = 2 * X.T @ (pred - y) / len(X)
        w_fast = w_fast - lr_inner * grad
    w_slow = w_slow + alpha_slow * (w_fast - w_slow)
    pred = X @ w_slow
    loss = np.mean((pred - y)**2)
    if outer_step % 5 == 0:
        print(f'Step {outer_step}, Loss: {loss:.4f}')

params.name: k
params.description: Number of inner optimizer steps before updating slow weights

params.name: alpha_slow
params.description: Interpolation coefficient for updating slow weights toward fast weights
